name: Open Vulnerability and Assessment Language
owner: Center for Internet Security (CIS) — originally MITRE, transferred to CIS in 2017
description: OVAL is an international, open standard for the structured assessment and reporting of machine state — used to describe how to check for the presence of vulnerabilities, configuration issues, patches, and inventoried software on a system. OVAL definitions are XML documents that name a test (e.g., "is patch X applied?") and describe how to evaluate it across supported platforms. OVAL was originally a MITRE project (with US-CERT/DHS sponsorship); maintenance transferred to CIS Center for Internet Security in 2017. The OVAL Repository and the OVAL Language specification continue at CIS.
relationToCloudSecurity: OVAL definitions underpin vulnerability scanning and configuration assessment for OS-level (Linux, Windows) cloud instances. Cloud workload protection platforms and CIS Benchmarks frequently consume OVAL.
relationToAISecurity: OVAL does not address AI/ML systems specifically. Applies to the underlying compute platform.

license.notes: OVAL is a community standard; CIS maintains it under terms permitting community use and contribution. Specific license details should be verified at cisecurity.org for any redistribution.

lifecycle.notes: Filed under cisecurity.org per the DNS-canonical rule — CIS is the publishing organization since 2017. Previously filed under mitre.org for historical continuity. See SecID redirect at secid:reference/mitre.org/oval -> secid:reference/cisecurity.org/oval.

current_extraction.notes: README-only stub. OVAL definitions are consumed directly from CIS by tools that use them.

notes: Reference entry only. OVAL is foundational to OS-level vulnerability scanning.
